title: Irish Eggplant Frittata
description: A savory, filling brunch dish made with eggs, eggplant, and Irish flair. Perfect for a weekend morning.
instructions: Brush the eggplant slices with melted Irish butter and season with salt and pepper.
In a large skillet, sauté the eggplant slices until golden brown, about 3-4 minutes per side.
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs and a pinch of salt. Set aside.
In a greased 9-inch (23cm) pie plate or skillet, arrange a layer of eggplant slices.
Pour half of the egg mixture over the eggplant, followed by half of the shredded Irish cheddar cheese.
Repeat the layers, starting with the eggplant, then the remaining egg mixture, and finally the remaining cheese.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the eggs are set and the cheese is melted and golden brown.
Remove from the oven and let it rest for 5 minutes before slicing and serving.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley and serve hot.
